events.raw_text:

Wed, Jun 18, 2025, 5:00 PM to Wed, Jun 18, 2025, 7:45 PM EDT

NCR - Global Headquarters

864 Spring St NW · Atlanta, GA

How to find us
dbt Global Circuit Series: Atlanta
M

Hosted by Mark S.

Atlanta dbt Meetup
4.6
28 reviews
Details

Whether you are new to dbt or an experienced user, this meetup is a great opportunity to gain insights, share experiences, and expand your network.

We’ll kick things off with a look inside how dbt is transforming NCR Voyix's data modeling processes as part of NCR's digital transformation journey, then dive into the latest tools and ideas dbt Labs has been shipping — what they unlock, how they fit together, and why we built them the way we did.

This meetup is designed for builders, thinkers, and tinkerers — folks who want to roll up their sleeves (figuratively), learn from each other, and imagine what’s next for the analytics engineering stack.

Whether you’re curious about what’s new, want to see how others are approaching similar problems, or just want to ask hard questions — you’re in the right place.

🤝Organizers: dbt Labs (Mark Shepherd) and Kun Zhu (NCR Voyix)
🏠Venue Host: NCR Voyix @ 864 Spring St NW (visitor parking is on 8th St until 6pm - see info below) Please arrive before 5:30 pm and keep the intense midtown traffic in mind!
🍕Catering: Refreshments and bites will be provided

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation:** https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

📝Agenda

5:00 - 5:30 | Check-in
5:30 - 6:00 | Presentation 1: Kun Zhu, VP of Data and AI/Chief Data Scientist @ NCR Voyix on "How dbt is Transforming our Data Modeling Processes on our Digital Transformation Journey" + Q&A
6:00 - 6:30 | Presentation 2: Cole Fraser, Solutions Architect, dbt Labs on "For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs” Q&A
6:30 - 6:55 | Presentation 3: Megha Panda, Data and Analytics Leader, HD Supply on "How adoption of dbt enabled HD Supply to improve velocity of data products" + Q&A
6:55 - 8:00 | Reception & Networking

EVENT DETAILS:
🚗 Free visitor parking. Please arrive by 6:30 pm. If you arrive after 6:30pm, the parking options will be different. Directions until 6:30

Turn onto Spring Street (one way)
Turn right onto 8th street
Visitor Parking is on your left
Go up ramp and turn left
Go down ramp and park anywhere

*If you arrive after 6:30, check your email for the phone # we sent you and call Alejandra for instructions on how get to the back of the building.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝 Join the #local-atlanta channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

***

***
Since 2016, dbt Labs has been on a mission to help data practitioners create and disseminate organizational knowledge. dbt is the standard for AI-ready structured data. Powered by the dbt Fusion engine, it unlocks the performance, context, and trust that organizations need to scale analytics in the era of AI. Globally, more than 60,000 data teams use dbt, including those at Siemens, Roche and Condé Nast.
Learn more at getdbt.com, and follow dbt Labs on LinkedIn, X, Instagram, and YouTube.

events.raw_text:

Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Atlanta dbt Meetup
4.6
28 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

Didn't make it to Vegas for Coalesce this year? Made it to Vegas and want to keep learning? Join the Atlanta dbt Meetup for a watch party where we will stream the most relevant sessions

🤝Organizer: Aimpoint Digital
🏠Venue Host: Aimpoint Digital, 7000 Central Parkway, Sandy Springs, GA 30328
🍕Refreshments: Pizza, Drinks
📝Agenda

Check-in & Registration + Welcome Remarks by Aimpoint Digital
30 min: Keynote - Turning data to value - A dbt customer panel - with Brandon Sweeney
30 min: Once more up the stack - Generative AI, the ADLC and the coming era of analytics engineering, with Jason Ganz
30 min: Late-stage transformations - Utilizing dbt Semantic Layer metrics - with Erica Louie
Discussion, Networking & Refreshments

dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

7000 Central Pkwy · Sandy Springs, GA

Atlanta dbt Meetup [In Person]
M

Hosted by Mark S. and 3 others

dbt L. is a Super Organizer

Atlanta dbt Meetup
4.6
28 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🏠Venue Host: 7000 Central Parkway, Sandy Springs, GA 30328
🍕Catering: To Be Announced
🤝Organizer: Aimpoint Digital

Please reach out if you have any dietary restrictions or allergies.

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

📝Agenda
● 4:45 - 5:25 | Check in/Registration
● 5:25 - 5:30 | Welcome Remarks by Aimpoint Digital
● 5:30 - 6:00 | Somnath Chateerjee and Brett Darcy (Cox Automotive) - Shifting Gears: How Cox Automotive Turbocharged Data Engineering with ELT
● 6:00 - 6:30 | Zach Lancaster (WBD) - From Core to Cloud
● 6:30 - 6:45 | Closing Remarks by Aimpoint Digital
● 7:00 - 8:00 | Reception

🗣️Presentation #1 - Shifting Gears: How Cox Automotive Turbocharged Data Engineering with ELT
Description - Migration from a modern ETL tool to a more conscious Data Engineering mindset with dbt. The reason, philosophy and strategy of the shift.
Speaker bios -
Somnath Chateerjee
In the dynamic realm of Data Engineering and Data Analysis at Cox, I have cultivated a robust expertise that spans Database Development, Data Modeling, and the nuanced dance between ETL & ELT processes. My role is not just about managing data; it is about sculpting it into a narrative that drives innovation and strategic growth.
Beyond the bytes and data points, I am a dedicated husband and an active father to a spirited 4-year-old, who ensures that life is never short of adventure and laughter. Balancing the equations of work and family life, I find solace in the simple pleasures of a good cup of coffee, the immersive escapism of movies, and the timeless allure of a well-told story.

Brett Darcy
As a Lead Software Engineer at Cox Automotive, I bring over a decade of expertise in the Business Intelligence (BI) domain, with a specialized focus on Dashboarding and Data Wrangling. My journey in the BI space spans 13 years, during which I have honed my skills in transforming complex data into actionable insights.
I hold a Master’s of Management Information Systems from Georgia College & State University (GCSU), where I deepened my knowledge of information systems management and advanced analytics.
When I’m not immersed in data or leading software development projects, I cherish beach trips with my family. As a father of five boys, aged 1 to 5 years old, these getaways with my wife and children are my favorite way to recharge and create lasting memories.

---

🗣️Presentation #2: From Core to Cloud at WBD

EVENT DETAILS:
The doors open at 4:45pm. Presentations begin at 5:30pm. Food and refreshments will be provided. Please RSVP and update status if unable to attend.

DIRECTIONS:
Feel free to park in the garage directly across from the entrance to the building. There is visitor parking on the first level. Additionally, the venue is walking distance from the Sandy Springs MARTA station. After entering the lobby through the glass front doors, continue past the two banks of elevators to the far hallway. Continue down the hallway and the large conference room will be on your right.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-atlanta channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------

dbt is an open source command-line tool that speaks the preferred language of data analysts everywhere—SQL. With dbt, analysts take ownership of the entire analytics engineering workflow, from writing data transformation code to deployment and documentation.
Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Photo by Christopher Alvarenga on Unsplash

Atlanta dbt Meetup [In Person]

Hosted by Brent B. and 2 others

Atlanta dbt Meetup
4.6
28 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🏠Venue: Mutation Brewing Company, 5825 Roswell Rd NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328 | Note this is not our usual venue!
Parking is available on-site.

🍕Catering: Food & Brews by Mutation Brewing!

🤝Organizer: Aimpoint Digital

Please reach out if you have any dietary restrictions or allergies.

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

📝Agenda

● 4:45 - 5:00 | Check in/Registration

● 5:00 - 5:15 | Welcome Remarks by Aimpoint Digital

● 5:15 - 5:30 | Speaker #1: Katherine Brock, dbt Labs

● 5:30 - 5:45 | Speaker #2: Brent Brewington, Aimpoint Digital

● 5:45 - 6:00 | Closing Remarks by Aimpoint Digital

● 6:00 - 9:00 | MDS Tacky Sweater Party

🗣️Presentation #1: Coalesce Recap | Katherine Brock
Speaker Bio: Katherine Brock is a Senior Solutions Architect at dbt Labs, brings extensive experience in data and analytics and is skilled in tools like dbt, SQL, Databricks, Azure, AWS, and Salesforce. Her career path includes significant roles at Slalom and Deloitte Consulting, complemented by a Bachelor of Science in Systems Engineering from the University of Virginia. With a strong background in applying data analysis to enhance business processes and patient care, highlighted by her project work at MD Anderson Cancer Center, Katherine is passionate about leveraging technology to drive efficiency. She is looking forward to sharing her insights from the dbt Coalesce 2023 conference at the upcoming Atlanta dbt meetup.

---

🗣️Presentation #2: Ugly Sweaters, Elegant Code: A Holiday Special on DataOps with dbt | Brent Brewington
Speaker Bio: Brent is a Principal Data Engineering Consultant at Aimpoint Digital with 10 years of experience in the analytics & data engineering space​. Prior to joining Aimpoint, Brent worked as a Data Engineering-focused Data Scientist at Home Depot where he pioneered the tooling and platform for dbt, running on BigQuery. His dbt work in Services Data Science led to several other teams adopting it: Enterprise ML Ops, Enterprise Item Data Management, and Canada Data & Analytics teams​. He has worked on data and project management teams across Manufacturing & Retail, spanning Field Sales Operations, Contact Center Operations, Supply Chain, Accounting, and Quality Management and is excited to share his holiday special on DataOps ith dbt.

EVENT DETAILS:

Doors open at 4:30pm. Presentations begin at 5:00pm. Food and refreshments will be provided. Please RSVP and update status if unable to attend.

Make sure to rsvp for both the Meet-Up and the Tacky Sweater Party if you plan to attend both!

🧥 MDS Ugly Sweater Party Registration : https://www.getdbt.com/resources/sweater

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/

🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-atlanta channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------

dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

🍕Catering: Chinese-Indian Fusion with assorted beers, wines, and cocktails

🤝Organizer: Aimpoint Digital

Please reach out if you have any dietary restrictions or allergies.

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

📝Agenda

● 4:45 - 5:25 | Check in/Registration

● 5:25 - 5:30 | Welcome Remarks by Aimpoint Digital

● 5:30 - 6:00 | Speaker #1: Mahtab Rahman, Cox Automotive

● 6:00 - 6:30 | Speaker #2: Grant Cloud, PrizePicks

● 6:30 - 6:45 | Closing Remarks by Aimpoint Digital

● 6:30 - 8:00 | Reception

🗣️Presentation #1: Mahtab Rahman
Speaker bio: Mahtab Rahman is Sr. Director of Architecture at Cox Automotive, a global automotive services and software company whose mission is to transform the way the world buys, sells, owns, and uses cars. He is responsible for the scalable and sustainable development and delivery of data and analytics capabilities throughout the enterprise.

Mahtab studied computer science at Georgia State University, and currently lives in Atlanta with his wife Esther and their beloved fur giant Lucy. Mahtab often finds inspiration not only in the ubiquity of lean patterns, but also how they yield delicious sushi during his travels to Japan, which feed into his personal mission: optimizing the flow and use of information throughout society.

---

🗣️Presentation #2: Grant Cloud
Speaker bio: Grant Cloud is the Senior Director of Data Strategy at PrizePicks, where he leverages data to inform business strategy, align business operations, and make real-time decisions to drive responsible growth. As the team’s lead, Cloud oversees the data engineering, data science & business intelligence teams. With experience in both corporate and startup environments, Cloud has been responsible for data-driven insights that has yielded significant impact across organizations such as Turner Sports, the Atlanta Falcons, Atlanta United and the Georgia Tech Baseball Team. Cloud is originally from Atlanta, Georgia and holds a Bachelor of Science in Industrial Engineering from the Georgia Institute of Technology.

Outside of work, Cloud is an avid sports enthusiast who enjoys playing and watching a wide range of games, including pickleball, spikeball, golf, baseball, basketball, and football. He is particularly passionate about college football and PGA. He is an active member of the local sports community and often plays on various recreational teams. Cloud has been a lifelong fan of Georgia Tech athletics, the Atlanta Braves and the Atlanta Falcons.

EVENT DETAILS:

Doors open at 4:45pm. Presentations begin at 5:30pm. Food and refreshments will be provided. Please RSVP and update status if unable to attend.

DIRECTIONS:

Feel free to park in the garage directly across from the entrance to the building. There is visitor parking on the first level. Additionally, the venue is walking distance from the Sandy Springs MARTA station. After entering the lobby through the glass front doors, continue past the two banks of elevators to the far hallway. Continue down the hallway and the large conference room will be on your right.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/

🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-atlanta channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------

dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Atlanta dbt Meetup [In Person]

Hosted by Alaisha A. and Brent B.

Atlanta dbt Meetup
4.6
28 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🏠Venue Host: 7000 Central Parkway, Sandy Springs, GA 30328
🍕Catering: Light bites with assorted beers, wines, and cocktails
🤝Organizer: Aimpoint Digital

Please reach out if you have any dietary restrictions or allergies.

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

📝Agenda
● 4:45 - 5:25 | Check in/Registration
● 5:25 - 5:30 | Welcome Remarks by Aimpoint Digital
● 5:30 - 6:00 | Tim Castillo (Dagster): Using Your Usage: Tips for Optimizing for Cloud Spend
● 6:00 - 6:15 | Nate Nunta (dbt Labs): dbt Product Roadmap
● 6:15 - 6:45| Mike Sandt (Salesloft): Data Modernization - Not Just a Technical Problem
● 6:45 - 7:00 | Closing Remarks by Aimpoint Digital
● 7:00 - 8:00 | Reception

🗣️Presentation #1: Using Your Usage: Tips for Optimizing for Cloud Spend

Speaker bio: Tim Castillo is a passionate and data-driven Data Engineer & Developer Advocate for Dagster, specializing in optimizing cloud spend. With experience in ETL/ELT, workflow automation, and data governance, Tim enjoys discussing engineering abstractions, SQL, Python, and iterative process improvement. When not immersed in data, you can find Tim sharing insights at the gym, practicing Muay Thai, exploring the newest food pop-up, or spending time with his beloved cats.

🗣️Presentation #2: dbt Product Roadmap

Speaker bio: Nate Nunta is a product manager at dbt Labs. He works on dbt Cloud offerings, including Cloud IDE and dbt-docs. Prior to joining dbt Labs, he built a data team and used dbt for a few years. He is an engineer by training and is based in the Bay Area. He loves building things, cooking/baking/eating, and overanalyzing movies and TV shows.

🗣️Presentation #3: Data Modernization - Not Just a Technical Problem

Summary: In this talk, Mike will discuss the strategies used at Salesloft to improve their Data team’s agility and ability to respond to changing business needs. While this presentation will touch on the technical capabilities that create these advantages, there will be a heavy slant toward processes and team topologies that enable this type of behavior. Much of this is influenced by Mike's experience as a software engineer and the lessons he believes data teams can learn from their Product/Engineering teams.

Speaker bio: Mike Sandt is a former software engineer turned data practitioner. He currently leads the Data & Analytics team at Salesloft. When not working with data Mike is an avid ice hockey player & golfer.

EVENT DETAILS:
Doors open at 4:45pm. Presentations begin at 5:30pm. Food and refreshments will be provided. Please RSVP and update status if unable to attend.

DIRECTIONS:
Feel free to park in the garage directly across from the entrance to the building. There is visitor parking on the first level. Additionally, the venue is walking distance from the Sandy Springs MARTA station. After entering the lobby through the glass front doors, continue past the two banks of elevators to the far hallway. Continue down the hallway and the large conference room will be on your right.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-atlanta channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------

dbt is an open source command-line tool that speaks the preferred language of data analysts everywhere—SQL. With dbt, analysts take ownership of the entire analytics engineering workflow, from writing data transformation code to deployment and documentation.
Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🏠Venue Host: 7000 Central Parkway, Sandy Springs, GA 30328
🍕Catering: Rumi's Kitchen
🤝Organizer: Aimpoint Digital

Please reach out if you have any dietary restrictions or allergies.

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

📝Agenda
● 4:45 - 5:25 | Check in/Registration
● 5:25 - 5:30 | Welcome Remarks by Aimpoint Digital
● 5:30 - 6:00 | Brent Brewington (Home Depot): Using dbt to drive DataOps on a Data Science balanced team
● 6:00 - 6:30| Aaron Reese (Chick-fil-A): Modernizing Data Engineering at CFA
● 6:30 - 6:45 | Closing Remarks by Aimpoint Digital
● 7:00 - 8:00 | Reception on the balcony

🗣️Presentation #1: Using dbt to drive DataOps on a Data Science balanced team

Description: Walking through how we use dbt & DataOps in Home Depot Services Data Science to take a systematic, modular approach to data for ML. We take a foundation-first approach in the AI Hierarchy of Needs, using dbt to build the foundational data so Data Scientists can “SELECT * FROM some_ideal_clean_and_pristine.table_that_you_think_exists”, to minimize need for data cleaning in Pandas. We’re a balanced team of data engineers, data scientists, and product managers, building Data, ML, and Analytics products that feed into systems & reporting to drive action by our field sales & install workforce.

Speaker bio: Meet Brent Brewington, a data scientist at Home Depot working on building analytics and ML products for Outside Sales & Install Operations. Passionate about Lean Mgt and DataOps, Brent started the company's #data-engineering channel and hosts a monthly book club. Outside of work, Brent plays hockey & cello, enjoys backpacking trips, and loves to travel and try new restaurants with his wife.

---

🗣️Presentation #2: Modernizing Data Engineering at CFA

Description: Learn how dbt is helping CFA bring engineering velocity and increased collaboration to analytics initiatives.

Speaker bio: Aaron Reese serves in Chick-fil-A’s Digital Transformation and Technology department as an Enterprise Architect focused on Advanced Analytics. He’s responsible for putting the tools in the toolbox that make up the analytics ecosystem and has a strong passion for enabling high-performing teams to deliver with speed and excellence.

EVENT DETAILS:
The doors open at 4:45pm. Presentations begin at 5:30pm. Food and refreshments will be provided. Please RSVP and update status if unable to attend.

DIRECTIONS:
Feel free to park in the garage directly across from the entrance to the building. There is visitor parking on the first level. Additionally, the venue is walking distance from the Sandy Springs MARTA station. After entering the lobby through the glass front doors, continue past the two banks of elevators to the far hallway. Continue down the hallway and the large conference room will be on your right.
